rationale.R1.2: The section contains a material logical inconsistency: RadAbstain is claimed to achieve 'the strongest published result on this benchmark to date' at 87.4% AUROC @ 50% coverage, while the immediately preceding paragraph cites Liu and Zhang (2019) at 91.2% AUROC @ 50% coverage on the same dataset. These premises and the conclusion cannot both be true; logical-consistency review is central regardless of delegation intent.
rationale.R1.3: The numeric overclaim (87.4% > 91.2%) is also an evidence-claim alignment failure—the stated result does not support the comparative superiority claim. Any substantive help with this section must surface this.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly positions RadAbstain as a delta over prior ensemble-based work on ChestX-ray14, making novelty characterization central. The framing of the contribution (single-pass vs. K-fold inference cost) and the benchmark comparison both require novelty scrutiny.
rationale.R1.6: If the user's intent is prose-level editing rather than substantive review, writing polish would be the load-bearing dimension. The delegation 'help me with this section' admits this reading equally.
rationale.R1.7: The section makes specific quantitative claims attributed to Liu and Zhang (2019) and Wang et al. (2017). Citation accuracy and the question of whether Liu and Zhang (2019) actually exists as described are load-bearing given the numerical inconsistency.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you asking for substantive feedback on logical consistency, evidence-claim accuracy, and citation integrity (which includes a critical numerical discrepancy in the text), or for writing-quality and prose-style improvements only?
